redacted: The live 'Account:' row carried the recording operator's ChatGPT login. It is replaced byte-for-byte with 'user@example.com' (16 chars in, 16 chars out), so every column of the box renders exactly as captured. This is the ONLY edit to bytes.raw.
notes: Hand-recorded probe completing the read-only presentation family: -s read-only -a on-request renders 'Read Only (Ask for approval)'. Note the family is NOT uniform in how it spells the approval policy — untrusted and never appear verbatim, on-request is prettified to 'Ask for approval' (matching the workspace family's 'Workspace (Ask for approval)'). That is exactly why the table is an exhaustive lookup of observed strings and never a parsed <sandbox>,<policy> pair. Widths are measured from the rendered 120x40 screen. widest_status_row_cols/status_box_outer_cols is the box's rendered width; intrinsic_row_cols is the minimum unwrapped width each row needs (opening bar + label column + value + closing bar), which is what CODEX_STATUS_MIN_COLS has to clear. NOTE the Session: row needs 62 cols, above the current CODEX_STATUS_MIN_COLS = 60 — a documented FOLLOW-UP to raise the constant with this evidence, not a defect here: both the session scrape and the permission parse anchor the closing bar on the same physical line, so a wrapped row fails closed at any width.
